# scroll-zoom-thing

> CSS 3D perspective parallax for MkDocs Material — no JS, pure CSS

## What it is

A minimal reference implementation of the parallax hero used in MkDocs Material. Uses `perspective`, `translateZ`, and `scale()` to create smooth depth-based scroll parallax without any JavaScript.

## Key concepts

- `perspective` on the scroll container establishes the 3D rendering context
- Each layer uses `translateZ(N)` to sit at a different depth
- Layers further from the viewer scroll slower — natural 3D projection
- `scale()` compensates for depth-induced size reduction
- No scroll events, no JS — pure browser 3D geometry

## CSS variables per layer

- `--md-parallax-depth` — depth value; higher = slower scroll; suggested ladder: 8, 5, 2, 1
- `--md-image-position` — horizontal crop position as a percentage

## Skills

Agent skills for this project:

- **setup-parallax** — scaffold the layer structure
- **generate-prompts** — generate AI image prompts for each parallax layer
- **convert-images** — convert images to AVIF at correct dimensions
- **tune-layers** — adjust depth and crop per layer for visual balance

## Files

- [docs/assets/stylesheets/home.css](docs/assets/stylesheets/home.css)
- [docs/overrides/home.html](docs/overrides/home.html)
- [mkdocs.yml](mkdocs.yml)
- [skills/](skills/)
- [.claude/commands/](.claude/commands/)
